title: Discontent
content:
آ آ آ آ LAURENCE RABY.

آ آ آ آ Laurence:
آ آ آ آ I said to young Allan Mï؟½Ilveray,
آ آ آ آ Beside the swift swirls of the North,
آ آ آ آ When, in lilac shot through with a silver ray,
آ آ آ آ We haulï؟½d the strong salmon fish forth,
آ آ آ آ Said only, ï؟½He gave us some trouble
آ آ آ آ To land him, and what does he weigh?
آ آ آ آ Our friend has caught one that weighs double,
آ آ آ آ The game for the candle wonï؟½t pay
آ آ آ آ Us to-day,
آ آ آ آ We may tie up our rods and away.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ I said to old Norman Mï؟½Gregor,
آ آ آ آ Three leagues to the west of Glen Dhu,
آ آ آ آ I had drawn, with a touch of the trigger,
آ آ آ آ The best bead that ever I drew,
آ آ آ آ Said merely, ï؟½For birds in the stubble
آ آ آ آ I once had an eye, I could swear
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s down, but heï؟½s not worth the trouble
آ آ آ آ Of seeking. You once shot a bear
آ آ آ آ In his lair,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis only a buck that lies there.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ I said to Lord Charles only last year,
آ آ آ آ The time that we toppï؟½d the oak rail
آ آ آ آ Between Whartonï؟½s plough and Whynneï؟½s pasture,
آ آ آ آ And clearï؟½d the big brook in Blakesvale,
آ آ آ آ We only, at Warburtonï؟½s double
آ آ آ آ He fell, then I finishï؟½d the run
آ آ آ آ And killï؟½d clean, said, ï؟½So bursts a bubble
آ آ آ آ That shone half an hour in the sun,
آ آ آ آ What is won?
آ آ آ آ Your sire clearï؟½d and captured a gun.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ I said to myself, in true sorrow,
آ آ آ آ I said yestereï؟½en, ï؟½A fair prize
آ آ آ آ Is won, and it may be to-morrow
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twill not seem so fair in thine eyes,
آ آ آ آ Real life is a race through sore trouble,
آ آ آ آ That gains not an inch on the goal,
آ آ آ آ And bliss an intangible bubble
آ آ آ آ That cheats an unsatisfied soul,
آ آ آ آ And the whole
آ آ آ آ Of the rest an illegible scroll.ï؟½
meaning: About: Adam Lindsay Gordon was an Australian poet, jockey and politician.
